threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think a 'forest' in London means a bunch of trees, not just a decked-out pub with fairy lights.
Caught a group of teens on the 333 reenacting scenes from a horror film, complete with a fake blood prop. Only in Brixton after dark.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found a stray cat lounging on the U-Bahn seat, unbothered, while commuters squeezed past it. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a public pool where the beer is cheaper than the entry fee. It's a real summer vibe at the Spree!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're local if they casually mention how many times they've dodged a pigeon while waiting for the 14, like it’s a rite of passage.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 6 bus today, and the driver was blasting '80s hits. Everyone actually smiled. Felt like a mini block party amid the usual chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every café has Wi-Fi and a power outlet. In Gràcia, some places don’t even have chairs—just sit on the ground and enjoy your coffee.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still ask for a 'tapa' with everything—seriously, locals just dive into the joint plates and call it a day.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'train' and not the 'overground'—everyone here gives you that look when you do.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded the driver with a ukulele. Oddly, the whole bus joined in for 'Wonderwall'—felt like a scene from a movie.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was in Neukölln when I saw a guy in a suit buying a döner while juggling his laptop and a skateboard. Only here, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see bars turning their beer taps off during a BVG strike, as if to say, 'No transport, no party.' A weird but telling dance of local life.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they order a burrito and instinctively ask for extra salsa, but still carry around a reusable straw.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo waiting for the 14, adjusting his bow tie as a pigeon crapped on his shoe. Classic SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss that locals don’t really hang out in the Plaça del Sol after sunset; it’s more of a morning coffee spot for us.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their struggle with the 'horitzó' in menus – it’s not just a view, it's a linguistic hurdle at cafes.
